title: memory_health exceeds its token budget and only emits a hint
evidence: Command: node .opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs memory_health --json '{}' --warm-only --format json --timeout-ms 10000. Observed: hints=["Response exceeds token budget (1234/1000)"], meta.tokenCount=1247, meta.tokenBudget=1000. Source: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1270-1314 only truncates data.results arrays; memory_health returns a diagnostic object at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:1220-1258.
detail: The dispatcher detects the over-budget envelope, but when the payload has no truncatable data.results array it only appends a warning and still returns the oversized response. So the token budget is advisory for memory_health, not enforced.
fix_sketch: Add a compact health projection or per-tool budget reducer for non-results envelopes before final serialization.

title: memory_health advertised budget disagrees with runtime budget
evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:404-406 advertises memory_health Token Budget: 800; .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/architecture/layer-definitions.ts:59-67 assigns L3 tools including memory_health tokenBudget 1000; runtime command above returns meta.tokenBudget=1000.
detail: Public tool schema/docs and enforcement metadata disagree on the budget callers should plan around. This makes the observed overflow confusing because the response exceeds both 800 and 1000, while the hint only reports 1000.
fix_sketch: Use one source of truth for per-tool budgets and generate schema descriptions from it.

title: First-call session priming can dominate memory_health envelopes
evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:818-853 appends session priming hints and stores the full sessionPrimeContext in meta.sessionPriming; .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/hooks/memory-surface.ts:458-517 returns enriched constitutional memories plus primePackage under a 4000-token hook budget. First probe observed hints including "Session priming: loaded 10 constitutional memories" and "Response exceeds token budget (3248/1000)", with meta.tokenCount=3260.
detail: First-call priming is useful bootstrap context, but it is injected after tool dispatch into a low-budget health response. Callers asking for a quick status can receive several thousand tokens of priming metadata before the health payload stabilizes.
fix_sketch: For low-budget diagnostic tools, attach only priming counts and a pointer/summary, or run priming through the target tool's final envelope budget.
